title: Session Bundle
description: Unified YAML/JSON bundle containing all data for a game session. Import via the Session Bundle Importer in Settings to populate all stores at once.

x-import-info.notes: The session bundle is the recommended way for AI agents to prepare a full game session. Creatures/hazards defined in the bundle are imported first, then encounters can reference them by 'creatureId' or 'creatureName'. Unresolved references produce warnings but do not block import.

name.description: Session name, e.g. 'Session 12 - The Vault of Stars'

description.description: Brief session description for GM reference

partyLevel.description: Default party level for encounters in this session

creatures.description: Custom creatures used by this session. Imported before encounters so they can be referenced.

hazards.description: Custom hazards used by this session.

encounters.description: Encounters for the session. Creatures can be referenced by creatureId/creatureName.

creatureId.description: Reference a creature by its ID (from the creatures array or existing store)

creatureName.description: Reference a creature by name (fuzzy matched against all loaded creatures)

hacking.description: Hacking encounters with computer stat blocks

starship.description: Cinematic starship scene templates. The shape mirrors SavedScene from starship-scenes.schema.json — see that file for the full field reference. Important fields the runner exercises: starship.bonuses (clickable skill chips), starship.templateId (link to a saved ship template), threats[].routine (per-turn actions with rollable attackBonus / damage / dc), threats[].immunities/weaknesses/resistances, threats[].specialAbilities, starshipActions[] grouped by role.

victoryCondition.description: Drives the inline progress chip in the scene header. defeat → 'Threats N / M active'; victory_points → '+/-/+2' counter with vpRequired target; survival → 'Survive N / M rds'; escape → 'in progress'; custom → renders customCondition text.

customCondition.description: Required for custom scenes — short label rendered in the header.

roleDescriptions.description: Flavour text per role for the GM to read aloud.

availableRoles.description: Role ids PCs can occupy (per GM Core p.231). Standard set: captain, engineer, gunner, magic_officer, pilot, science_officer. Repeats are allowed (e.g. ['gunner', 'gunner'] for two gunner slots).

roles.description: Pre-assigned role/PC pairings for this scene. The InitiativeRollModal uses these to prefill rows; falls back to the active party from partyStore if empty.

starship.description: PC starship stats. See starship-scenes.schema.json#/definitions/Starship for the full shape. Skill bonuses (e.g. {'Piloting': 2}) render as click-to-roll chips on the ship card.

threats.description: Enemy ships, hazards, environmental dangers. See starship-scenes.schema.json#/definitions/StarshipThreat for the full shape. The runner promotes routine attack/skill_check actions to a top-level 'Attacks & Checks' row on each card with click-to-roll bonuses, damage, and DCs.

starshipActions.description: Crew actions available to PCs. See starship-scenes.schema.json#/definitions/StarshipAction. Grouped by role in the Crew Actions reference list.

starshipTemplates.description: Reusable PC starship templates — see starship-templates.schema.json. Loading a template into a scene sets scene.starship.templateId so the GM can save the configuration once and reuse it across encounters. Templates marked isCampaignShip persist post-scene HP/Shields back so damage carries forward.

shops.description: Saved shop snapshots, including rolled inventory and shopkeeper. Items persist as full objects so the same inventory comes back on import (no re-rolling). The legacy params-only shape ({name, shopType, settlement, partyLevel}) is still accepted for backward compatibility but will be skipped with a warning by the importer.

shop.description: Full GeneratedShop snapshot — see /schemas/shops.schema.json for the complete shape (inventory, shopkeeper, levelRange, itemCount).

shopType.description: Legacy: dropped on import. Present in old bundles only.

settlement.description: Legacy: dropped on import. Present in old bundles only.

partyLevel.description: Legacy: dropped on import. Present in old bundles only.
